input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed in Android 11+ and .NET MAUI has been evolving its approach. Let me provide guidance based on what I know.

In .NET 10 (and starting with .NET 9 updates), the Android `Resize` soft input mode became unreliable because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`. The recommended approach now is to use edge-to-edge display with `WindowInsets` handling.


For .NET MAUI on .NET 10, there are two main paths: either set the soft input mode to `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_NOTHING` and manually handle keyboard visibility by listening to `WindowInsets` to adjust padding, or leverage the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior that's been available since .NET 8, which automatically scrolls content when the keyboard appears. The `Resize` mode was already obsolete on Android 30+ starting in .NET 9, so the framework has been pushing toward these alternatives.

For a chat-style layout specifically, `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` might not handle the bottom input bar perfectly, so manually detecting the IME inset using `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` on Android and adjusting the layout's bottom padding is often more reliable. Alternatively, structuring the layout with a Grid where the input bar naturally moves up as the keyboard appears can work well too. Setting up keyboard handling with WindowInsetsCompat...
